scenario: Your star striker has been offered a massive contract by a rival club. He says he wants to stay, but the money is life-changing.
optionA: Sell him for a huge transfer fee.
optionB: Break the wage structure to keep him.

scenario: The board has arranged a lucrative, but exhausting, mid-season friendly in another continent.
optionA: Publicly question the wisdom of the trip.
optionB: Rest your key players and focus on youth.

scenario: A prominent fan group is planning a protest against your 'boring' style of football, even though you're getting results.
optionA: Invite the leaders for a private discussion.
optionB: Release a statement defending your methods.

scenario: A key player is caught in a nightclub the night before a big game. The media is having a field day.
optionA: Drop him from the squad entirely.
optionB: Start him to show your faith in him.

scenario: Your team has been hit by a nasty virus, ruling out half of your first team for the next match.
optionA: Request the league to postpone the match.
optionB: Field a team of youngsters and reserves.

scenario: The club's new crypto sponsor is demanding you feature their logo more prominently. The fans hate it.
optionA: Agree and wear a giant bitcoin on your training top.
optionB: Politely decline and risk the sponsorship deal.

scenario: Your star striker has released a controversial tell-all autobiography, upsetting the dressing room.
optionA: Fine the player and make a public statement.
optionB: Downplay the issue and handle it internally.

scenario: A sudden heatwave has made the pitch unplayable. The league is forcing you to play anyway.
optionA: Complain to the media about player safety.
optionB: Quietly rotate your squad to use players with better stamina.

scenario: A key player is having a nightmare game. He's a fan favorite.
optionB: Give him until 60 minutes to improve.

scenario: The opposition changes to a formation that's causing your team problems.
optionB: Stick with your original game plan and trust your players.

scenario: You're dominating possession but can't seem to create any clear chances.
optionA: Switch to a more direct, long-ball attacking style.
optionB: Stay patient and trust the chances will come.

scenario: Your captain is constantly arguing with the referee and is close to getting a second yellow card.
optionA: Substitute him to avoid going down to 10 men.
optionB: Tell him to calm down but keep him on the pitch.

scenario: A viral TikTok video shows your players mocking your training drills behind your back.
optionA: Make the players involved issue a public apology.
optionB: Laugh it off in the press, calling it 'team bonding'.
